methods.description: Process for collecting and preparing sediment cores from various sites for experimental analysis.

steps.description: At each sampling site, four replicate, 15 cm deep sediment cores were collected in 19 cm (i.d.) clear, acrylic benthic chambers at random locations along a 15 m long transect running parallel to the water's edge.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment cores using benthic chambers along transect

steps.description: Immediately after collection, all cored sediments were covered with 25 cm of top water and transported to Waikiki Aquarium where they were placed in a temperature controlled water bath (24°C).
steps.searchable_summary: transport and store sediment cores in temperature-controlled water bath

steps.description: Sediments were allowed to settle, and conditions in each chamber allowed to equilibrate for 24 h prior to the start of the experiments.
steps.searchable_summary: allow sediment cores to settle and equilibrate for 24 hours

methods.description: Culturing and labeling of Chlorella spp. algae for use as a food source in experiments.

steps.description: Phytoplankton was cultured for 3 weeks at 24°C under a 16:8 h light: dark cycle in artificial seawater modified with F/2 algal culture medium.
steps.searchable_summary: culture phytoplankton in modified seawater under light cycle

steps.description: Labeled by replacing 25% of 12C bicarbonate in the culture medium with NaH13CO3.
For harvest, inocula were concentrated by centrifugation (404× g, 5 min), washed 5 times in an isotonic solution to remove excess labeled bicarbonate, and freeze dried.
steps.searchable_summary: concentrate and wash algae, then freeze dry

methods.description: Setup of experimental chambers with labeled algae for ecosystem functioning studies.

steps.description: 13C-labeled Chlorella spp. were injected into three of the four chambers (T =0 h) and stirrers mounted 5 cm beneath the chamber lids were activated to homogeneously distribute the algae over the enclosed sediment surface.
steps.searchable_summary: inject labeled algae into chambers and activate stirrers

steps.description: Approximately 1.8 g algal-C m−2 was added to each experimental chamber except to cores collected at the KBC site, where ∼1.6 g algal-C m−2 was added.
steps.searchable_summary: add specific amount of algal-C to chambers

steps.description: Ten minutes after the algae were injected; stirrers were switched off for 1 h to allow the labeled algae to sink to the sediment surface.
steps.searchable_summary: allow algae to settle on sediment surface

steps.description: Stirrers were then turned on and the chamber waters gently mixed (without creating sediment resuspension) at 3 rpm for the remainder of the experiments.
steps.searchable_summary: gently mix chamber waters at 3 rpm

methods.description: Procedures for analyzing sediment and bacterial samples for various parameters including SMB, POC, and bacterial biomass.

steps.description: Frozen sediment sub-core samples were thawed, sieved with freshwater on nested 250 µm and 1 mm mesh sieves, the mangrove leaf litter, roots and fibrous material picked out at 150 × magnification under a dissecting microscope and then dried at 35°C for 4 days.
steps.searchable_summary: thaw, sieve, and dry sediment samples

steps.description: Dried material was weighed on an electronic balance.
Frozen sediment samples for bacterial analysis were freeze dried and subsequently ground with a mortar and pestle.
steps.searchable_summary: freeze dry and grind sediment samples

steps.description: Lipids were extracted from approximately 3 g of dried sediment by a Bligh and Dyer extraction procedure.
steps.searchable_summary: extract lipids using Bligh and Dyer method

steps.description: The lipid extract was then derivatized to volatile fatty acid methyl esters (FAME) and measured by gas chromatography isotopic ratio mass spectrometry (GC-IRMS) for PLFA concentration and δ13C-signatures.
steps.searchable_summary: derivatize lipids and measure by GC-IRMS

methods.description: Sampling and analysis of chamber water for oxygen and DIC fluxes.

steps.description: Water samples used to calculate fluxes of oxygen (sediment O2 consumption) and DIC were extracted with a 140 ml syringe from each chamber at 12 h intervals throughout the duration of each 48 h study.
steps.searchable_summary: extract water samples for oxygen and DIC analysis

steps.description: Simultaneously, 140 ml of replacement water from a freshly filled, filtered seawater reservoir was added to each chamber.
Two 60 ml samples of chamber water were immediately transferred into gas-tight glass B.O.D. bottles and fixed for dissolved O2 Winkler analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: transfer and fix water samples for Winkler analysis

steps.description: A 2 ml water sample was passed through a 0.25 µm sterile, cellulose acetate filter into gas tight 4 ml borosilicate glass vials and fixed with 5 µl of 6% HgCl2 for total DIC and 13C isotope-ratio mass-spectrometry (IRMS) analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: filter and fix water samples for DIC and IRMS
